findings.title: North Star Metric: User Engagement
findings.description: The North Star metric for SmartNotify is the Daily Active Users (DAU), which measures the number of unique users engaging with the notification system daily. This metric reflects overall user engagement and satisfaction with the product.
assumptions: Users engage with the product daily to receive notifications.
recommendations: Monitor DAU trends to assess user engagement over time.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Notification Click-Through Rate (CTR)
findings.description: The Notification Click-Through Rate (CTR) measures the percentage of notifications that users interact with. This is a key input metric indicating the effectiveness of notification content and delivery.
assumptions: Higher CTR indicates better user engagement with notifications.
recommendations: Optimize notification content to improve CTR.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Unsubscribe Rate
findings.description: The Unsubscribe Rate measures the percentage of users who opt out of receiving notifications. A lower unsubscribe rate indicates higher user satisfaction with the notification system.
assumptions: Users unsubscribe due to notification fatigue or irrelevance.
recommendations: Analyze reasons for unsubscribes to improve user retention.

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: Average Notifications per User
findings.description: The Average Notifications per User measures the average number of notifications sent to each user. This metric should not increase significantly to avoid notification fatigue.
assumptions: Users prefer a manageable number of notifications.
recommendations: Limit average notifications to maintain user engagement.

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: 95th Percentile Delivery Latency
findings.description: The 95th Percentile Delivery Latency measures the time taken to deliver notifications to users. This metric should not exceed the target to ensure timely notifications.
assumptions: Users expect timely delivery of notifications.
recommendations: Reduce delivery latency to improve user experience.

findings.title: Measurement Gap: User Preference Data
findings.description: There is insufficient data on user preferences regarding notification timing and content, which is critical for optimizing the ML model for notification batching.
assumptions: User preferences significantly impact notification engagement.

evidence.excerpt: ML model should learn per-user preferences for delivery timing

recommendations: Implement user surveys or feedback mechanisms to gather preference data.

findings.title: Experiment Opportunity: Notification Content Testing
findings.description: Conduct A/B testing on different notification content styles to determine which formats yield higher CTR and lower unsubscribe rates.
assumptions: Different content styles will have varying impacts on user engagement.
recommendations: Run A/B tests with a minimum sample size of 1000 users per variant.

summary: The analysis identifies the North Star metric as Daily Active Users (DAU) and outlines primary KPIs including Notification CTR and Unsubscribe Rate. Guardrail metrics focus on Average Notifications per User and Delivery Latency. Gaps in user preference data highlight areas for improvement, and an A/B testing opportunity is recommended to enhance notification engagement.
